claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex. Since he's 17, I need to ask a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? For example, is he studying for A-levels, a BTEC, NVQ (Level 3 or below), or enrolled on an approved training course?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! Since Alex is 17 and studying for A-levels, that's considered approved education for Child Benefit purposes.

Now I have a couple more questions:

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you pay for things like his food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I pay for everything - his food, clothes, and all his expenses. He lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you. And one final question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex is 17 years old and lives with you
- He is in approved education (studying A-levels)
- You are responsible for him (paying for his food, clothes, and accommodation)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to tell you that **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

**For Alex:**
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ You are responsible for Alex (you pay for his food, clothes, and accommodation)
- ✅ Alex lives with you
- ✅ Although Alex is 17 (over 16), he is staying in approved education (A-levels), which means you can continue to receive Child Benefit until he's under 20, as long as he remains in approved education or training
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with making a Child Benefit claim. Please note that once Alex finishes his A-levels or leaves approved education, you'll need to inform the Child Benefit Office as your eligibility may change at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which is over 16, but he is staying in approved education (studying A-levels). According to the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. A-levels count as approved education. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (pays for his food, clothes, and accommodation), Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident who is responsible for their 17-year-old son Alex, who lives with them and is currently in approved education (A-levels). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

According to the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. A-levels count as approved education. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (pays for his food, clothes, and accommodation), Alex lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK resident who is responsible for their 17-year-old son Alex, who lives with them and is currently in approved education (A-levels). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
